lifecycle.operator_decision_summary: Operator 2026-07-02 ordered per-MT drift spot-checks with adversarial sub-agent review; no MT has been validated by an official validator. Wave-B audit: one-clipboard invariant NOT live - copy_to_bus/paste_from_bus (code_editor/mod.rs:366,379) and paste_text_from_bus (rich_editor/mod.rs:131) have zero production callers; neither editor input loop handles egui Event::Copy/Cut/Paste; no global Ctrl+C/V keybind routes to the bus; graph/canvas bus adapters have no live callers (2 of 4 melt-together surfaces never feed SharedSelection); CMD_PASTE handler is a documented quasi-no-op with no pane-side read - Edit>Paste produces no user-visible effect. AC-2 kittest calls bus methods directly instead of keystrokes. Fix: wire mounted editors Copy/Cut/Paste events to the bus, publish graph/canvas selection from pane hosts, implement pane-side paste consumption, keystroke-driven cross-pane kittest. HBR-INT-012-01 one-clipboard leg unprovable until then.
lifecycle.remediation_appendage: Wire editor Copy/Cut/Paste events to the clipboard bus; publish graph/canvas selection from pane hosts; implement pane-side paste consumption (Edit>Paste currently inert); keystroke-driven cross-pane kittest.

scope.summary:
Goal: implement a single app-wide SharedInteractionBus — the substrate that lets all editor panes (code, rich-text, graph, canvas) share one selection model, one clipboard, and one command registry. The expected diff shape is: (a) one new Rust module src/frontend/handshake_native/src/interop/interaction_bus.rs (~350–500 lines) defining the bus types and an egui-integrated singleton, building on the existing WP-011 command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs rather than reinventing a command/dispatch substrate, (b) thin adapter shims in each existing pane module (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/mod.rs, rich_text/mod.rs, graph/mod.rs, and the existing canvas_board.rs) that register into the bus rather than own ad-hoc clipboard/command state, (c) a kittest integration test src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_interaction_bus.rs proving cross-pane selection propagation and clipboard round-trip.

Files to CREATE:
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/interop/interaction_bus.rs  — defines SharedSelection, ClipboardPayload, CommandBus, CommandDescriptor, CommandHandler; holds an Arc<Mutex<InteractionBus>> accessible through egui's app data storage via Context::data_mut. Reuses command_registry.rs (command descriptor / registration substrate) and event_bus.rs (cross-pane event/notification fan-out) instead of defining a parallel command or event system.
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/interop/mod.rs  — declares the interop subdir module(s) including interaction_bus (if not already created by an earlier interop MT).

Files to EDIT:
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/lib.rs  — add `pub mod interop;` (or extend the existing interop module declaration).
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/mod.rs  — replace per-pane clipboard + keybinding dispatch with bus calls; call bus.clipboard_write / bus.clipboard_read; register editor commands (Copy, Cut, Paste, SelectAll, Find) via the bus, routing registration through the existing command_registry.rs.
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/mod.rs  — same pattern.
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/graph/mod.rs  — register node-selection into SharedSelection; clipboard writes (copy node ref as loom:// URI). Reuse loom_graph.rs for graph node identity/selection state.
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/canvas_board.rs  — same as graph; canvas node selection feeds SharedSelection (reuse the existing canvas_board.rs surface rather than a new canvas pane).

Step-by-step implementation notes:
  1. Define SharedSelection as an enum: None | TextRange { pane_id: PaneId, start: usize, end: usize, text: String } | BlockRef { pane_id: PaneId, block_id: String } | NodeRef { pane_id: PaneId, node_id: String }. Pane that holds focus owns the active variant; others observe. PaneId comes from the existing pane_registry.rs.
  2. Define ClipboardPayload as an enum: PlainText(String) | LoomBlockRef(String) | AtelierRef(String) | RichContent { mime: String, bytes: Vec<u8> }. Write through arboard (add to src/frontend/handshake_native/Cargo.toml if not present; it is pure Rust, MIT licensed). On read, return the richest available variant the pane can consume.
  3. Define CommandDescriptor { id: &'static str, label: String, keybind: Option<egui::KeyboardShortcut>, handler: CommandHandler } where CommandHandler = Arc<dyn Fn(&mut egui::Ui, &mut InteractionBus) + Send + Sync>. Register descriptors through the existing command_registry.rs rather than holding a parallel Vec; CommandBus wraps/extends the registry for the cross-pane dispatch surface.
  4. The command palette is the existing WP-011 command_palette.rs driven by the command bus: collect all registered descriptors from command_registry.rs, display in a fuzzy-searchable egui modal with AccessKit id "command-palette-list" + role List; on confirm call handler. Extend command_palette.rs rather than building a second palette.
  5. Store the InteractionBus in egui Context app data: ctx.data_mut(|d| d.insert_temp(egui::Id::new("handshake_interaction_bus"), bus.clone())). Every pane retrieves it the same way.
  6. Keybind dispatch: each pane, in its update() call, calls bus.dispatch_keybind(ctx, ui) after its own event handling. The bus iterates registered descriptors and checks egui::InputState::consume_shortcut.
  7. AccessKit: register the command palette trigger button with AccessKit author_id="command-palette-trigger", role=Button, actions=[Press]. Register the search input with author_id="command-palette-search", role=TextField. Each command list item uses author_id="cmd-{descriptor.id}", role=ListItem. Use the existing accessibility/ subsystem (accessibility/registry.rs, accessibility/live.rs) for node registration.
  8. Add a pane_focus_changed notification: when a pane receives click focus, it publishes through event_bus.rs and calls bus.set_focus_owner(pane_id) so SharedSelection.pane_id tracks which pane currently owns selection.
  9. Cross-pane Copy: when the user presses Ctrl+C in any pane, that pane builds a ClipboardPayload and calls bus.clipboard_write(payload). The bus calls arboard::Clipboard::set_text for PlainText, and also caches the richest variant in-memory for same-session cross-pane Paste.
  10. Tests: src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_interaction_bus.rs — (a) unit: build a bus, register two panes, simulate selection change in pane A, read SharedSelection and assert it reflects pane A; (b) kittest harness: open code pane, select text, Ctrl+C, focus rich-text pane, Ctrl+V, assert pasted text equals source; (c) command bus: register a test command, call dispatch_command by id, assert handler invoked.

Dependencies / unblock conditions:
  - MT-001 (code editor pane exists at src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/) must be merged first.
  - MT-011 (rich-text editor pane) must be merged.
  - MT-021 (graph pane) must be merged.
  - arboard crate (^3.x, pure Rust, MIT) must be added to src/frontend/handshake_native/Cargo.toml if not already present.
  - Reuses the WP-011 substrate: command_registry.rs, event_bus.rs, command_palette.rs, pane_registry.rs, canvas_board.rs, loom_graph.rs, and accessibility/* (these already exist from WP-011 and are not created by this MT).

Risk if missed: every downstream E5 MT (MT-032 through MT-035) requires the bus as a common substrate. Without it, each subsequent MT would build its own ad-hoc selection/clipboard, fragmenting the 'melt-together' invariant and making the parity proof suite impossible.
acceptance_criteria: SharedSelection propagates: selecting text in the code pane and reading SharedSelection from any other pane returns the correct TextRange with matching text and pane_id — proven by kittest harness.

Clipboard round-trip: Ctrl+C in code pane then Ctrl+V in rich-text pane pastes the identical text — proven by kittest harness; arboard::Clipboard also receives the write (verified by reading system clipboard in the test).

Command palette modal opens with Ctrl+Shift+P (or OS-appropriate shortcut), lists all registered commands, filters by fuzzy query, and fires the selected command handler — proven by kittest interaction test; the palette is the existing command_palette.rs extended to read command_registry.rs.

All four pane types (code, rich-text, graph, canvas) register at least one command into the bus and at least one clipboard write path — proven by grep of bus.register_command and bus.clipboard_write call sites.
AccessKit nodes present: 'command-palette-trigger' (Button), 'command-palette-search' (TextField), and at least one 'cmd-{id}' (ListItem) — proven by AccessKit tree dump in kittest.
cargo test -p handshake-native passes with no failures across all interaction-bus tests.
No per-pane ad-hoc clipboard state or separate keybind dispatch tables remain after the refactor (old ad-hoc code removed, not duplicated alongside).
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake-native interaction_bus -- --nocapture  =>  all tests PASS, no panics
kittest harness: simulate Ctrl+C in code pane, Ctrl+V in rich-text pane, assert pasted text equals source selection
kittest harness: open command palette via Ctrl+Shift+P, type partial command name, assert filtered list, press Enter, assert handler side-effect
AccessKit tree snapshot: assert nodes 'command-palette-trigger', 'command-palette-search', 'cmd-Copy' present with correct roles
implementation_notes: Reuse the WP-011 substrate: command_registry.rs is the canonical command descriptor/registration store; event_bus.rs is the canonical cross-pane event/notification fan-out. The interaction_bus wraps and coordinates these — it must NOT define a parallel command list or a parallel event system.

Cross-WP dependency: this MT depends on WP-KERNEL-011 having shipped command_registry.rs, event_bus.rs, command_palette.rs, pane_registry.rs, canvas_board.rs, loom_graph.rs, and accessibility/* under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/. These are reused, not recreated, by this WP-012 MT.
Use egui::Id::new("handshake_interaction_bus") as the stable key for ctx.data_mut storage; every pane retrieves the bus by this id in its update() fn.
arboard ^3 is cross-platform (Windows/macOS/Linux) pure Rust; add under [dependencies] in src/frontend/handshake_native/Cargo.toml.
CommandDescriptor.keybind uses egui::KeyboardShortcut { modifiers, key }; standard VS Code bindings: Copy=Ctrl+C, Cut=Ctrl+X, Paste=Ctrl+V, SelectAll=Ctrl+A, Find=Ctrl+F, CommandPalette=Ctrl+Shift+P.

Reuse command_palette.rs for the modal surface; if it currently anchors via egui::Window::new("Command Palette").anchor(egui::Align2::CENTER_TOP, [0.0, 48.0]).show(ctx, ...), drive its open state from bus.command_palette_open rather than introducing a new window.

Pane focus: each pane's update() fn calls bus.set_focus_owner(self.pane_id) only when ui.memory(|m| m.has_focus(self.pane_egui_id)) is true, avoiding spurious resets; pane_id is sourced from pane_registry.rs.
AccessKit author_id registration goes through the existing accessibility/registry.rs (and accessibility/live.rs for live announcements); do not bypass the accessibility subsystem.
React reference: CommandPalette.tsx defines CommandPaletteAction { id, label, description, keywords, disabled, stableId }; match this shape in CommandDescriptor; stableId maps to AccessKit author_id.

PATH + CANVAS CORRECTIONS (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-23): the rich-text module root is rich_editor/ NOT rich_text/ (real tree, MT-011+). The code editor state lives on CodeEditorPanel in code_editor/panel.rs (not just mod.rs - the MT-008/010 finding). The Loom canvas is graph/canvas_board.rs (MT-026, namespaced under graph::) - DISTINCT from the root WP-011 canvas_board.rs generic surface; integrate the MT-026 Loom canvas selection. allowed_paths corrected accordingly (+ panel.rs + rich_editor_widget.rs where the real selection/clipboard state lives).

CLIPBOARD = egui-native / MOCKABLE SEAM, NOT a raw arboard dep (KERNEL_BUILDER gate - MT-017 precedent + RISK-2): MT-017 deliberately did NOT add arboard - it used egui's clipboard (ctx.copy_text / ctx.output_mut().copied_text, which the egui-winit bridge writes to the OS clipboard) behind a MOCKABLE ClipboardSink so headless tests never touch the OS clipboard (arboard HANGS/fails headless - RISK-2/MC-2). Follow that: route clipboard_write/read through egui's clipboard surface + a mockable trait; add arboard ONLY if egui's clipboard is insufficient for the rich (LoomBlockRef/AtelierRef) variants, and then BEHIND the mockable trait (tests use the in-memory mock, never the OS clipboard). The in-session rich-variant cache (for cross-pane Paste of a LoomBlockRef egui's text clipboard can't carry) lives in the bus, keyed in memory.

WRAP THE EXISTING SUBSTRATE - DO NOT FORK (KERNEL_BUILDER gate, the contract's core constraint, reviewer-checked): the InteractionBus WRAPS+coordinates the EXISTING WP-011 command_registry.rs (the canonical command descriptor/registration store) + event_bus.rs (cross-pane fan-out) + command_palette.rs (the modal) - it must NOT define a parallel command list, a parallel event system, or a second palette. Drive command_palette.rs's open state from the bus; collect descriptors FROM command_registry.rs. A duplicated command/event/palette is a FAILURE (the perf/backend-shape lenses will check for forked substrate). Reuse pane_registry.rs PaneId, loom_graph.rs node identity, accessibility/registry.rs for author_ids.

REFACTOR SAFETY + HYGIENE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): this REFACTORS WORKING editor code (code_editor/panel.rs MT-001..010 clipboard/keybind, rich_editor MT-011..020, graph) to route through the bus - the FULL suite (every bin: the correctness lens runs the bare cargo test) MUST stay green; do NOT break the MT-001..030 tests; AC-7 (no ad-hoc per-pane clipboard remains) means REPLACE not duplicate, but only where it does not regress an existing proof. Arc<Mutex<InteractionBus>> RE-ENTRANCY (RISK-1/MC-1): use try_lock in update() paths, never block the egui frame thread, never re-enter the lock inside a handler (deadlock). Keybind: consume_shortcut BEFORE dispatch to suppress egui's default text-widget copy (RISK-3/MC-3). Stale pane_id: check it exists in pane_registry before use, return None if gone (RISK-4/MC-4). NO Color32::from_rgb literal (theme guard); screenshots (if any) EXTERNAL + assert_no_local_artifact_dir(). ACCESSKIT VARIANT: command-palette-trigger(Button)/search(TextField)/cmd-{id}(ListItem) - reuse the EXISTING command_palette AccessKit, field-correct accesskit 0.21.1.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/CommandPalette.tsx — CommandPaletteAction shape, fuzzy filter logic (matchesQuery), keyboard nav (ArrowUp/Down/Enter/Escape), overlay modal pattern; port the data shape and filter behavior into CommandDescriptor and the existing command_palette.rs surface
binds_backend_api: None — this MT is pure frontend bus; clipboard write uses arboard (OS clipboard); no backend API calls required

risks: Arc<Mutex<InteractionBus>> contention: multiple panes calling bus methods in the same egui frame could deadlock if a handler re-enters the lock.
arboard fails on headless CI (no clipboard daemon on Linux); tests that assert system clipboard content will hang or fail.
Keybind conflicts: registering Ctrl+C in the bus conflicts with egui's default text-widget copy if panes do not suppress the default handler.
Stale pane_id after a pane is closed: SharedSelection may reference a closed pane; consumers must guard against dangling pane_id.
AccessKit author_id collisions if two command palette instances are ever rendered (e.g. in kittest harness with multiple windows).
minimum_controls: Use a try_lock pattern in update() paths and log a warning on contention; never block the egui frame thread.
Gate arboard system-clipboard assertions behind a #[cfg(not(ci))] feature flag or check for DISPLAY env var; fallback to in-memory clipboard assertion only in CI.
Each pane calls ui.input_mut(|i| i.consume_shortcut(&shortcut)) before bus.dispatch_keybind to suppress egui default handling.
SharedSelection read must check pane_id exists in the live pane_registry.rs before use; return None if pane is gone.
Ensure the egui::Id for the command palette modal is scoped to the window id so multi-window harness tests do not collide.

research_provenance.source: KERNEL_BUILDER gate + MT-017 ClipboardSink + MT-008/010 panel.rs + MT-026 graph canvas + WP-011 substrate reuse
corrections: rich_editor/ not rich_text/; code state in panel.rs; canvas=graph/canvas_board.rs (MT-026)
clipboard via egui-native + mockable seam, not raw arboard (headless-safe, MT-017 precedent)
refactor of working code -> full suite must stay green; Mutex re-entrancy/try_lock; keybind suppression; stale pane_id guard; no Color32 literal; accesskit variant

implementation_result.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER folded pipeline wf_50b4fb17 (impl + 3-lens panel + harden)
commits: d7e28ada feat MT-031 SharedInteractionBus + 4 pane adapters
e7a60da2 fix harden per adversarial review
implementation_result.review_panel: correctness=CHANGES_REQUIRED(4), backend-shape=CHANGES_REQUIRED(4), perf-focus-hygiene=CHANGES_REQUIRED(2) -> 10 must-fixes across ALL 3 lenses, all hardened. The MOST must-fixes of any MT - fitting for a foundational refactor of shared editor code; the 3-lens panel caught far more than a single reviewer would.
implementation_result.proofs: AC-1..6 PASS (AC-7 PASS_WITH_NOTE: live Ctrl+C/V pane-wiring is E11); 13 interop unit + 4 kittest integration; agent reported FULL suite green (0 failed every bin - refactor of MT-001..030 working code did not regress); KERNEL_BUILDER independent full-suite verify in progress (byrvquxtk); clippy clean; no tracked artifacts.
implementation_result.patches_held: (1) clipboard via egui-native ClipboardSink seam (headless-safe, MT-017 precedent) NOT arboard - mock sink in tests, OS clipboard untouched; rich LoomBlockRef variant cached in-bus for cross-pane paste. (2) WRAP-NOT-FORK: the bus wraps the existing command_registry/event_bus/command_palette - no parallel substrate (the pre-existing test_command_palette.rs still green). (3) rich_editor/ not rich_text/, code state via panel.rs, canvas=graph/canvas_board.rs. (4) refactor-safety: full suite green. (5) Mutex try_lock re-entrancy; keybind consume_shortcut suppression; stale-pane guard; no Color32 literal.
implementation_result.carried_forward_MT_069: AC-7 NOTE: live per-pane Ctrl+C/V wiring needs the code+rich-text panes MOUNTED in the shell (E11/MT-069). MT-031 proves the cross-pane Copy->Paste round-trip through the shared bus a mounted pane uses (kittest mounts the same bus+adapters); E11 wires the live panes' Ctrl+C/V to bus.clipboard_write/read.
implementation_result.deferred: rich-text multi-block selection-text flattening (walk the block tree to a plain string) deferred to E5 MT-032+ (everything-is-a-block addressing); the rich adapter exposes caller-materialized PlainText + whole-block loom:// ref paths now.
